issue_library.title: Wrong date confirmed — day-of-week mismatch (off by 7 days)
issue_library.root_cause: Weekday-relative resolution used `(target_dow - today.weekday()) % 7 or 7`, turning modulus 0 into 7 — booking next week instead of today. Also: 'heute' / today's date handled inconsistently across parser paths. Result: user says 'Montag 18. Mai' but bot confirms '25. Mai'.

A1.7_D2: '22. Mai' error — fragment from other utterance parsed as reservation date
A1.8_D3: WOCHENTAG_FALSCH alongside DATUM_FALSCH in 3/7 scenarios

issue_library.fix_target: server/brain/conversation_state.py date resolution + server/brain/v4_pipeline.py readback builder

issue_library.title: 'nächsten Samstag' mapped to nearest Saturday instead of following-week Saturday
issue_library.root_cause: Both reservation_workers._parse_date and conversation_state treat 'nächsten <weekday>' as the next upcoming occurrence, not the occurrence in the NEXT calendar week. If today is Saturday, 'nächsten Samstag' = +7 days but current code returns +0 or +7 inconsistently.
observed_examples: A1.6_D2: 'nächster Samstag' resolved to 23. Mai instead of 30. Mai
D5_D5: 'next week' resolved to 16. Mai instead of 23. Mai
confirmed_working_fixes: For 'nächsten <weekday>': always add 7 days AFTER finding the next occurrence of that weekday, ensuring it's strictly next week
Add _resolve_dates_in_goal() to caller bot goals so scenario dates stay in sync with resolution logic

issue_library.title: Multi-date utterances: first day-name match overwrites intended reservation date
issue_library.root_cause: _parse_date used search() returning the FIRST day name match. In utterances like 'Bestellung für Samstag und Tisch für nächsten Freitag', 'Samstag' (catering) overwrote 'Freitag' (table reservation) via _persist_resolved_entities_to_state.
observed_examples: commit 9b94290: 'philipp stress test' utterance with two dates — first match won
confirmed_working_fixes: Prefer date occurring AFTER reservation keywords ('Tisch für', 'reservier', 'buchen')
Fall back to findall[-1] (last day name) if no reservation keyword precedes any date
Applied in commit 9b94290

issue_library.title: Bot repeats 'Was darf ich für Sie tun?' indefinitely
issue_library.root_cause: Two sub-causes: (a) intent classifier returns UNKNOWN for valid reservation/query intents on repeated utterances, causing fallthrough to default greeting; (b) confirmation utterances like 'Ja, das stimmt so' not mapped to CONFIRM intent — treated as new unrecognized input, resetting the flow. Also: _reservation_finalized flag checked AFTER profile routing, not before.
observed_examples: A2.1_D1: 98.2/100 baseline → 40.2 after A1 code changes (regression)
A1.7_D2: phone asked at turns 8, 14, 20, 26 — 4 achtung flags
A1.9_D3: 'Was darf ich für Sie tun?' loop despite reservation intent stated

issue_library.title: Phone number asked multiple times even after user confirms or declines
issue_library.root_cause: phone_number_user_provided flag not propagated across turns. Also: phone extraction used test_only=True mode on some paths which prevents the flag from being set. Also: 'unter' (German verbal separator, e.g. '026 unter 3457978') missing from _PHONE_CONNECTORS, causing digit scanner to break early.
observed_examples: A1.7_D2: phone asked at turns 8, 14, 20, 26
A1.8_D3: phone extraction used test_only mode — flag never set
commit eea7a57: '026 unter 3457978' broke because 'unter' not in _PHONE_CONNECTORS — bot looped ~99s until caller hung up

issue_library.title: Name contamination: 'Anna reservieren' extracted as ('Anna', 'Reservieren')
issue_library.root_cause: re.IGNORECASE made [A-ZÄÖÜ] match lowercase. Pattern 'auf den Namen X Y' would match 'Anna reservieren' → ('Anna', 'reservieren') → capitalized to 'Anna Reservieren'. Also: 'Weiter' (German: proceed) captured as surname → 'Philipp Weiter'.

transcript 90ed704f: live call demo-7dcdb47b6bea showed 'Philipp Weiter' as captured name
confirmed_working_fixes: Require BOTH captured tokens to start with uppercase in the ORIGINAL utterance (not after re.IGNORECASE lowering) — commit eea7a57
Add common German verb infinitives to _NAME_BLOCKLIST: reservieren, buchen, bestellen, stornieren, notieren, anrufen, anfragen, machen, sagen, gehen, kommen, legen (commit eea7a57)
Expand _NAME_BLOCKLIST with command words: weiter, bitte, danke, ja, nein, okay, gut, schön
Guard surname assembly: only fire if bot EXPLICITLY asked for Nachname in previous turn

issue_library.title: Weather intent silently dropped in multi-intent turns (weather + reservation)
issue_library.root_cause: Weather only fetched when profile was 'business_info'. In combined utterances like 'Wie ist das Wetter und ich möchte reservieren', reservation keywords dominated → profile='reservation_start' → weather intent silently dropped.

issue_library.title: TinyGenerator missing max_tokens parameter — every LLM call fails, bot returns fallback phrase
issue_library.root_cause: Anthropic API call missing required max_tokens=512. Every LLM call fails silently. Bot always returns hardcoded fallback phrase ('Ich helfe Ihnen gerne.'). Fixed in commit 8a7dfba.
observed_examples: commit 8a7dfba fix #1: TinyGenerator max_tokens missing → caused every call to fail
confirmed_working_fixes: Add max_tokens=512 (or appropriate value) to all Anthropic/Gemini API calls in TinyGenerator and tier2_runner.py (commit 8a7dfba)

confirmed_working_fixes: Add patterns 'reserviert|bestätigt|gebucht' → reservation_confirmed gate

issue_library.title: Tool calls not firing despite clear user intent (orders, reservations, menu lookups)
issue_library.root_cause: Bot collects user intent (order, reservation, menu query) but never calls the corresponding tool. v4_pipeline.py slots may be filled but the commit gate is not triggered — either COMMIT_TOOLS_REQUIRED_SLOTS demands more slots than are realistically available, or the intent classification returns SLOT_FILL instead of COMMIT on the turn where all required slots are present. For pickup orders: phone_number is NOT required but was previously listed in COMMIT_TOOLS_REQUIRED_SLOTS['create_order']. Fix: ensure commit gate fires when order_items + customer_name are present (phone optional for pickup). For reservations: availability + name + party_size + date + time must all be present.
observed_examples: G2.2_D4: No tools fired despite clear reservation request (next Friday, 19:00, 2 persons, Schmidt). score=46
I2.1_D3: No tools fired on menu/vegetarian questions. score=35
H2.2_D3: No order tool despite clear Bibimbap order request. score=41
I2.2_D4: No tools for price/billing queries. score=27
I1.2_D4: No order tool for clear takeout request (2x Bibimbap, name Koch). score=34
confirmed_working_fixes: Remove phone_number from COMMIT_TOOLS_REQUIRED_SLOTS['create_order'] — pickup orders don't need phone (applied 2026-05-19)
Lower commit gate: if order_items + customer_name present → fire create_order even without phone (pickup default)
Check _is_all_slots_filled() logic in v4_pipeline.py — ensure it counts ALL required slots, not just missing ones

issue_library.title: Bot loops asking for phone number after user already provided name + items
issue_library.root_cause: In v4_pipeline.py dup-utterance handler: when customer_name and order_items are filled but phone is not, the bot asks for phone. For pickup (Abholung) orders, phone should be optional. The dup-utterance handler must detect pickup keywords (abholung, abholen, hole ich ab, mitnehmen) and skip phone collection. Additionally, the phone extraction regex may be matching date/time fragments as phone numbers.
observed_examples: F2.1_D2: BOT_LOOP — bot asked for phone 3+ times, RUFNUMMER_FALSCH flag, score=42
F2.2_D3: BOT_LOOP — identical phone question repeated. Score=53
G1.1_D2: BOT_LOOP on phone turn 5/7. Score=49
confirmed_working_fixes: Added pickup detection in dup-utterance handler (v4_pipeline.py): check for abholung/abholen keywords and order_type=='pickup' before asking phone (applied 2026-05-19)
In dup-utterance handler: count phone asks with state._phone_ask_count; after 2 asks, gracefully end without phone

issue_library.title: Wrong name or phone confirmed in readback (NAME_FALSCH / TELEFON_FALSCH flags)
issue_library.root_cause: Name extraction accepts German weekdays (Montag, Samstag) or date fragments as customer_name. Phone extraction may match date/time numeric fragments ('18 2026', '19:00' → '1 9 0 0'). In readback, bot uses the incorrectly extracted value. Root fixes: (1) Reject German weekday/month names in name extraction. (2) Validate extracted phone: min 7 digits, no pure date patterns (YYYY-MM-DD, HH:MM). (3) Store confirmed slots in state.confirmed_name / state.confirmed_phone separately from extracted.
observed_examples: F2.1_D2: NAME_FALSCH (Braun expected, 'Ihrem Namen' confirmed) + RUFNUMMER_FALSCH. Score=42
G1.2_D3: hallucinated names/phones ('Samstag' as name). Score=57
H1.3_D3: KEIN_READBACK + 'Bekam' instead of 'Bibimbap'. Score=46. 9 achtung flags
confirmed_working_fixes: Reject German day-of-week names (Montag, Dienstag, ..., Sonntag) in _extract_name_from_utterance
Reject German month names (Januar, ..., Dezember) in _extract_name_from_utterance
Phone validation: require >=7 digits and reject if matches date pattern (e.g. YYYY or HH:MM)
Store confirmed name in state.customer_name only after explicit user confirmation

issue_library.title: Missing complaint/refund/escalation tools — bot gives no resolution
issue_library.root_cause: Complaint scenarios (wrong order, refund request, price dispute) require tools like log_complaint, process_refund, escalate_to_manager. These tools either don't exist in the current tool set or v4_pipeline.py doesn't route complaint intents to them. Bot responds with generic text instead of taking action. Fix: in complaint.py, implement log_complaint() and process_refund() handlers. In v4_pipeline.py, detect 'beschwerde/complaint/rückerstattung/Erstattung' intent → route to complaint node.

issue_library.title: Bot stays silent / gives only greeting after user asks question
issue_library.root_cause: After the greeting turn, the bot fails to generate a follow-up response when the user asks a question. This causes the user to repeat the same question multiple times (user repetition loop). Causes: (1) FAQ node doesn't have handlers for discount/Stammkunde, competitor, vegetarian menu queries. (2) Intent detection maps unusual queries to UNKNOWN intent with no handler. Fix: (1) Add FAQ handlers for Stammkunde/discount, competitor redirect, vegetarian options. (2) Add fallback in UNKNOWN intent handler to ask clarifying question rather than stay silent.

In faq.py: add competitor redirect handler (politely decline, offer DOBOO alternatives)
In faq.py: add vegetarian menu handler (Bibimbap ingredients)

issue_library.title: Multi-intent not handled — bot ignores secondary request (pre-order + reservation)
issue_library.root_cause: When a user makes multiple requests in one utterance (e.g. 'reservieren + Bibimbap vorbestellen + Liefergebiet prüfen'), v4_pipeline.py only processes the first intent and discards the rest. The intent classifier returns a single IntentKind; secondary intents are lost. Fix: detect multi-intent utterances (multiple verbs/requests) and queue secondary intents in state for processing in subsequent turns.
observed_examples: G2.3_D5: reservation + Bibimbap pre-order + delivery query — only one processed. Score=53
G1.1_D2: Abholung + reservation confusion — secondary intent dropped. Score=49
confirmed_working_fixes: Detect 'und auch' / 'außerdem' / 'sowie' in user utterance → set state.pending_intents list
Process primary intent first, then check state.pending_intents for secondary processing

issue_library.title: Wrong opening hours returned for Saturday (16:00 instead of 18:00)
issue_library.root_cause: The get_opening_hours tool or the availability check tool returns 16:00 as Saturday opening time but the correct Saturday opening is 18:00. This is either hardcoded incorrectly or the tool fetches from a data source where Saturday config is wrong. Fix: find where Saturday opening hours are defined and correct to 18:00. Check tenant config, faq node, reservation node, and any hardcoded hour constants.

confirmed_working_fixes: Search for '16' or '16:00' in faq.py, reservation.py, and any tenant config; update Saturday to '18:00'

issue_library.title: Bot confirms wrong delivery type: says 'zur Lieferung' when customer said 'zum Mitnehmen/Abholen'
issue_library.root_cause: In `_build_pre_commit_order_summary_v4`, the delivery_clause logic uses `delivery_type` attribute first. If `delivery_type` is set to 'delivery' from a previous intent classification (e.g. IntentKind.DELIVERY), but `order_type` is 'takeaway' or 'pickup', the wrong clause is built. The HEAD code uses `getattr(state, 'delivery_confirmed', False) is False` which relies on Python identity comparison — `None is False` is False, so if delivery_confirmed is None (never set), it may fall through to 'Lieferung'. The safe fix: ensure delivery_clause defaults to ' zum Abholen' unless delivery is explicitly confirmed via both order_type AND delivery_confirmed.
observed_examples: G1.1_D2_skeptical: BESTELLART_FALSCH — erwartet: Abholung, bestätigt: Lieferung (attempt 1 baseline)

fix_attempts.description: Haiku changed _build_pre_commit_order_summary_v4 to default to ' zum Abholen' — also broke other order types
fix_attempts.outcome: regression — logic change broke delivery address scenarios
fix_attempts.lesson: Default to Abholen ONLY when order_type in (pickup, takeaway). Never change the elif for delivery.

confirmed_working_fixes: In _build_pre_commit_order_summary_v4: check `order_type in ('pickup', 'takeaway')` OR `delivery_confirmed is False` for Abholen. Check `order_type == 'delivery'` OR `delivery_confirmed is True` for Lieferung. DO NOT use `is False` on None — use `== False`.

issue_library.title: Haiku moves order pre-commit confirmation handler inside _order_slots_ok gate — creates deadlock
issue_library.root_cause:
In v4_pipeline.py, the pre-commit confirmation handler (lines ~998-1006 in baseline) is OUTSIDE and BEFORE the _order_slots_ok gate.
This is critical because _order_slots_ok requires `end_call_stage == 'idle'`, but during confirmation turn the stage is 'order_pre_commit_readback'.
When Haiku moves this handler INSIDE the `if _is_order_intent and _order_not_committed and _order_slots_ok:` block,
the confirmation handler can never be reached (deadlock): stage='order_pre_commit_readback' → _order_slots_ok=False → block skipped → confirmation never processed.
failed_fixes: Adding pre-commit confirmation handler inside `if _order_slots_ok:` block
Duplicating confirmation check blocks at different indentation levels
confirmed_working_fixes: Keep the pre-commit confirmation check at lines ~998-1006 OUTSIDE and BEFORE the _order_slots_ok gate
The pattern must be: check confirmation (update end_call_stage + _order_slots_ok) → then fall through to commit gate
NEVER nest the confirmation handler inside the `if _order_slots_ok:` block
issue_library.additional_notes:
Baseline correct structure:
  1. if end_call_stage in ('pre_commit_readback', 'order_pre_commit_readback'):
       if _is_confirmation_v4(user_text):
           state.end_call_stage = 'idle'; end_call_stage = 'idle'
           _order_slots_ok = _all_slots_present(...)  # recompute!
           # fall through to commit gate below
  2. if _is_order_intent and _order_not_committed and _order_slots_ok:
       # commit gate only reached if stage was reset to idle above

issue_library.description: conversation_state.py inline date parser doesn't handle ordinal month patterns ('ersten April') or übernächste woche before nächste woche check
issue_library.root_cause: update_state_from_utterance() checks 'nächste woche' before 'übernächste' (which is a substring of the latter), causing 'übernächste Woche Freitag' to match 'nächste woche' first (+7 days instead of +14 days). Also, German ordinal month phrases like 'ersten April' are not matched by any pattern, leaving state.reservation_date=None and triggering an infinite slot-ask loop.
confirmed_working_fixes: In update_state_from_utterance(), check 'übernächste' BEFORE 'nächste woche' in the elif chain (substring issue)
Add unified weekday+week-qualifier handler: compute 'this_monday + 7 (next) or +14 (übernächste)', then add weekday offset within that week
Add ordinal month pattern: _ORDINAL_DE_CS dict + regex matching 'ersten April' → date(year, month, day) with future-year advance if date < today
issue_library.notes: reservation_workers._parse_date() ALSO has the ordinal fix (applied previously) but conversation_state.py must ALSO be fixed because _all_slots_present checks state.reservation_date. If conversation_state.py doesn't set it, _all_slots_present() fails and the bot loops asking for the date even when _parse_date would return correctly.

issue_library.description: Party size not extracted from 'drei Personen' (German word-number without 'für' prefix)
issue_library.root_cause: The primary party_size regex requires 'für/zu' prefix: '(?:für|fuer|zu)\s+(\d|[a-zäöü]+)'. The fallback requires a digit: '\b(\d{1,2})\s+(?:person...)'. Neither matches 'drei Personen' (no 'für', and 'drei' is a word not a digit).
confirmed_working_fixes: Add third fallback: regex matching '(word_number) + Personen/Leute' without für prefix, using _WORD_NUMS_PARTY dict {eine:1, zwei:2, drei:3, ..., zwölf:12}
issue_library.notes: party_size=None causes _all_slots_present() to fail → bot asks 'Für wie viele Personen?' even though user already said 'drei'.

issue_library.description: Short FAQ scenarios (A2.1 'Wann habt ihr heute geöffnet?') show Grok score variance of 27-59 in consecutive identical runs
issue_library.root_cause: Short 2-3 turn conversations have high Grok variance. get_date_info is called (returns date/weekday, no opening hours). Bot uses business_info profile to answer. Score variance likely from Grok's subjective quality judgment on short responses + different persona reactions.
issue_library.notes: get_date_info does NOT return opening_hours — it only returns date+weekday. The bot must use the business profile (YAML config) for actual hour info. Must ensure the bot explicitly states the opening hours and end the call cleanly (should_end=True after FAQ answer). Higher Grok score requires: 1) explicit hour info in response 2) call ending cleanly.

issue_library.title: get_menu result stored as raw dict causes LLM to say 'Diese Information habe ich leider nicht'
issue_library.description: When get_menu is executed inline and the result is stored in ctx_doc.resolved_entities['menu_data'], the raw Python dict (e.g. {'hauptgerichte': [{'name': 'Bibimbap vegetarisch', 'price': 12.9, ...}]}) is injected into the LLM context. The LLM cannot parse this complex nested structure and falls back to 'Diese Information habe ich gerade leider nicht zur Hand.'
issue_library.root_cause: v4_pipeline.py lines 892-895 set ctx_doc.resolved_entities['menu_data'] = menu (raw dict). The context_doc_builder formats this as '  - menu_data: {large nested dict}' which the LLM cannot understand as menu information.
confirmed_working_fixes: Format menu dict as human-readable string in v4_pipeline.py before storing in resolved_entities: 'Speisekarte: Bibimbap vegetarisch: 12.90€ — ...' pipe-separated. FIXED 2026-05-20.
issue_library.notes: The YAML FAQ lookup at lines 914-954 provides nicely formatted strings, but it's only used when menu_data is NOT already in resolved_entities. After inline get_menu, menu_data IS set (to a dict), so the YAML lookup is skipped. Fix: convert the menu dict to a readable string at lines 892-895.

issue_library.title: Menu questions with 'Karte' or 'haben Sie' not detected — inline get_menu not triggered
issue_library.description: The _menu_content_kws list in v4_pipeline.py did not include 'karte' (as in 'auf der Karte'), 'haben sie', 'habt ihr' (without 'was' prefix). User phrasings like 'was Sie so auf der Karte haben' or 'haben Sie Bibimbap?' failed the keyword check, so inline get_menu was not pre-executed. The YAML FAQ fallback also missed these because the FAQ keywords didn't match.
issue_library.root_cause: _menu_content_kws at v4_pipeline.py line 779 was too narrow. Missing keywords: 'karte', 'menü', 'menu', 'haben sie', 'was für', 'welche gerichte', dish names like 'bulgogi', 'kimchi', 'mandu', 'tofu', etc.
confirmed_working_fixes: Added to _menu_content_kws: 'karte', 'menü', 'menu', 'habt ihr', 'haben sie', 'was für', 'welche gerichte', 'empfehlen', 'bulgogi', 'kimchi', 'mandu', 'tofu', 'japchae', 'tteokbokki', 'gericht', 'speise', 'sushi'. FIXED 2026-05-20.
issue_library.notes: Also expanded _dietary_kws in v4_pipeline.py to include: 'was habt ihr', 'was haben sie', 'was gibt es', 'was ist auf der karte', 'was für gerichte', dish names. This routes more menu questions to business_info profile (FAQ) before the ordering profile override takes effect.

issue_library.title: Caller bot knowledge (simplified YAML menu) doesn't match actual get_menu tool output
issue_library.description: phase_runner.py _load_doboo_knowledge() loaded menu from doboo.yaml menu.categories which has simplified entries like 'Bibimbap 14.90€'. But get_menu tool returns actual database menu with variants: 'Bibimbap vegetarisch 12.90€', 'Bibimbap Hähnchen 14.90€', 'Bibimbap Rind 16.50€'. When bot correctly reports 'Bibimbap vegetarisch kostet 12.90€', caller bot flags PREIS_FALSCH because it only knows about 'Bibimbap 14.90€'.
issue_library.root_cause: The menu.categories in doboo.yaml is a SIMPLIFIED VIEW for documentation only, not the actual tool data. The tenant database has the full detailed menu with all variants. phase_runner.py was using the wrong source.
confirmed_working_fixes: Updated phase_runner.py _load_doboo_knowledge() to load menu from server.core.tenant_config.get_tenant_registry().load_tenant('doboo').tool_data.menu — same source as the actual get_menu tool. Falls back to menu.categories if database unavailable. FIXED 2026-05-20.
issue_library.notes: CRITICAL: The caller bot must know about ALL menu variants to correctly verify the bot's responses. After this fix, both the bot and the caller bot use the same source of truth. The lru_cache on _load_doboo_knowledge must be cleared if menu changes mid-run (or restart the loop).

issue_library.title: get_cached_dish_price called without import in v4_pipeline.py — NameError crash
issue_library.root_cause: Haiku-generated order pre-commit readback code calls get_cached_dish_price(state, item) but does not import it. The function is defined in server.brain.conversation_state but was never imported at module level or locally in v4_pipeline.py. This causes a NameError whenever an order reaches the pre-commit readback stage.
issue_library.fix: Add module-level import at top of v4_pipeline.py: from server.brain.conversation_state import get_cached_dish_price as _get_cached_dish_price and update the call site to use the imported name. This ensures even if Haiku rewrites the local block, the module-level import remains.

issue_library.title: Phone number readback shows space-separated digits: "0 5 1 1..." instead of "0511..."
issue_library.root_cause: _build_pre_commit_summary_v4() uses " ".join(phone) for phone_clause when phone is a list. This produces space-separated digits like "0 5 1 1 3 3 9 7 6 6 5 4" instead of "051133976654". The Grok caller bot detects this as TELEFON_FALSCH.
issue_library.fix: Change phone_clause in _build_pre_commit_summary_v4 from f", Rückrufnummer {' '.join(phone)}" to f", Rückrufnummer {phone if isinstance(phone, str) else ''.join(str(d) for d in phone)}"

issue_library.title: Batch result files with None composite score prevent re-run when loop uses --resume
issue_library.root_cause: When a batch is started but fails (e.g. crash, timeout, API error), the loop creates a batch_result_*.json file with base_script_id and difficulty set but composite_score=None. On the next loop run with --resume, _load_completed() finds these files and adds their keys to the completed set, causing the loop to SKIP those batches forever. The batches are never retried despite not having passed.
issue_library.fix: Before starting a loop with --resume, delete all batch_result_*.json files where composite_score is None or 0.0. Command: python3 -c "import json, glob, os; [os.remove(f) for f in glob.glob(''/tmp/scenario_validation/batch_result_*.json'') if json.load(open(f)).get('composite_score') in (None, 0.0)]"

issue_library.title: ConversationState._last_user_text AttributeError crashes all scenarios with name collection
issue_library.root_cause: v4_turn_processor.py calls update_state_from_utterance(state, user_text) at step 1 BEFORE process_turn_v4 which is the only place that sets state._last_user_text = user_text. conversation_state.py line 2373 directly accesses state._last_user_text without getattr(), raising AttributeError on every call that reaches the bare-name detection path (bot asked for name + user replies with single capitalized word). This affects ALL reservation/order scenarios on first name-collection turn.
issue_library.fix: Use getattr(state, '_last_user_text', None) in conversation_state.py:2373 instead of state._last_user_text
symptoms: 'ConversationState' object has no attribute '_last_user_text' in text_run logs
0/7 personas passing in scenarios with name collection
All scenario attempts fail with this error in the scenario runner log

issues.title: Bot silently drops FAQ intent when reservation + menu price question in same turn
issues.root_cause: In v4_pipeline.py around line 654-660, when intent is UNKNOWN/FAQ, reservation keywords override profile to 'reservation_start'. This silently drops any FAQ (e.g. menu price) part of the turn. The caller then repeats the FAQ → BOT_LOOP.
issues.fix: Add multi-intent detection: if user text has BOTH reservation keywords AND menu price keywords ('was kostet', 'preis', etc.), inject a menu_price tool result before routing to reservation_start.

issues.title: TinyGenerator fails silently to 'Was darf ich für Sie tun?' when Anthropic API credits exhausted
issues.root_cause: TinyGenerator._call_llm() calls Anthropic client and raises exception when credits are low (HTTP 400). The exception propagates to TinyGenerator.generate() which catches it at line 267 and returns _FALLBACK_RESPONSE = 'Was darf ich für Sie tun?'. This causes every bot response to be the fallback greeting, triggering BOT_LOOP detection in ALL scenarios.
issues.fix: Added OpenAI gpt-4o-mini fallback in TinyGenerator._call_llm(): when Anthropic raises a credit-related error (400 with 'credit' or '400'/'403'), fall back to OpenAI gpt-4o-mini before returning empty string.

issues.title: GPT-4o fixer introduces undefined variable references (e.g. _is_price_discrepancy)
issues.root_cause: GPT-4o fixer modifies existing if-conditions to add new variable references (e.g. 'if _is_manager_request or _is_price_discrepancy:') without defining the new variable anywhere in scope. The ast.parse validation in fix_applier.py does NOT catch this since undefined variable references are runtime errors, not syntax errors. This causes NameError crashes in every turn.
issues.fix: Need runtime safety check: either (1) add pyflakes/pylint-style undefined-name check in fix_applier before applying, or (2) use restricted pattern matching to only allow variable names that already exist in the file.

issues.title: Bot responds with reservation apology text to manager escalation requests
issues.root_cause: H2.3 scenario: when user says 'Ich möchte mit dem Manager sprechen', the loop_complaint handler fires if 'manager' is in text. On first turn (repeat_count=1 < 2), the 'else' branch executes, checking for missing reservation slots. Since no reservation is active, _still_missing=[] and bot says 'Ich habe jetzt alle Angaben und lege die Reservierung sofort an' — completely wrong for a manager request.
issues.fix: Added explicit _MANAGER_KWS check BEFORE the loop_complaint handler. When manager request keywords detected and NOT a loop complaint, immediately respond with empathetic escalation text and set next_action='escalate'.
